query: What does the CSS padding property do in production?

competitors.label: padding
competitors.selectionReason: This is the canonical MDN reference for the padding property, including shorthand behavior, percentage padding, and how padding participates in the box model.
theirStrengths: MDN is authoritative on padding syntax, shorthand mapping, percentages, and box-model interaction.
theirGaps: The reference is less interview-shaped and less explicit about clickable-area growth and the common confusion between padding and pushing neighbors away.

realWorldUseCases: Padding controls the space inside an element, between its content and border. It increases the clickable/visual area without pushing neighboring elements away. Covers: CSS, padding, box model, layout, spacing.
followUpConfusion:
Overview

Padding is the internal space of a box, sitting between the content area (text, images, etc.) and the border. Increasing padding makes the element look larger because the background color and border expand outward to include that extra space.

realWorldUseCases: MDN defines padding thoroughly, but it is lighter on the concrete UI decision behind this property: grow the clickable area without creating outer separation.
actionableExamples: It includes canonical syntax and percentage examples, so the example criterion stays competitive.
followUpConfusion: Our page is more explicit about the follow-up confusion teams hit first: padding changes the box from the inside and can affect total size through the box model.

actionableExamples: Keep the shorthand-order note visible so the page stays practical without turning into a full box-model guide.

competitors.winReason: MDN is the canonical padding reference, but our page is stronger as an interview answer because it turns padding into a concrete inside-spacing, clickable-area, and box-sizing decision.
